title: Delicate Roast Vietnamese Provola Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Vietnamese dish featuring provola cheese, roasted to perfection in a tagine pot with a hint of mustard and a bouncy texture, perfect for a dinner meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together mustard seeds, fish sauce, lime juice, and olive oil.
Add the sliced provola cheese to the bowl and toss to coat with the mustard mixture.
In a large tagine pot, combine the coated provola cheese, chopped vietnamese coriander, minced garlic, and grated ginger.
Add the diced red bell pepper to the tagine pot and stir to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cover the tagine pot with a lid and roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to roast for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is lightly browned and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the tagine pot from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the delicate roast Vietnamese provola tagine hot, garnished with additional chopped vietnamese coriander if desired.
